corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 91-92 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 93-94 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Correspondence and Personal Papers, 1964-2016. II. Journals and Notebooks, 1964-2015. III. Writings, Research, and Project Files, 1945-2016. IV. Professional Papers, 1941-2016.

bioghist.head: Rachel Blau DuPlessis
bioghist.p: Rachel Blau DuPlessis is an award-winning American poet, essayist, editor, and feminist critic. Duplessis was born in Brooklyn, NY, taught at Temple University from 1974-2011, and her scholarly and creative works have been widely published since 1980. Her most well-known works include the "Drafts" series and H.D: The Career of that Struggle (1986).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Rachel Blau DuPlessis Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Rachel Blau DuPlessis Papers, which span from 1941-2016, contain writings, photographs, personal papers, and other documents by or related to Rachel Blau DuPlessis's life and work. Also contains extensive correspondence with other authors and poets including Susan Bee, Mei Mei Berssenbrugge, Johanna Drucker, and Forrest Gander.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Rachel Blau DuPlessis Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, writings, and other documentation related to Rachel Blau DuPlessis's life and work. Her extensive correspondence with other authors and poets includes letters from Susan Bee, Mei Mei Berssenbrugge, Johanna Drucker, and Forrest Gander.

scopecontent.p: Contains notes from Pound conference; Kootenay where DuPlessis presented "Midrash"; political poetry conference with Alan Gilbert, Teddy Harris, Tracie Morris, Kristen Prevallet; MLA poetry conference with notes on Charles Bernstein, Susan Howe, Jonathan Culler, Dubrow, R Greene, Ramazani, Barbara Guest, Helene Aji, Bob Perelman, Lyn Hejinian, Ron Silliman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials on "Threshold," "Blue Studios," and the publication of "Clay Songs as well as correspondence with Dan Bouchard, Evelyn Reilly, Ann Snitow, Michael Davidson, and final conversation with Barbara Guest. Notes on Barrett Watten.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes from Pound conference and Contemporary Literature conferences as well as materials from writing "Drafts" 74-76 and a review of Lee Friedlander.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes on "Drafts," as well as on Johanna Drucker at KWH conference, Cixous at Slought conference, Cole Swenson, and Frank Sherlock, C.A. Conrad, Jena Osman, and Jenn McCreary at a mini poetry conference.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes from Tubingen conference organized by Barrett Warren.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials related to the publication of "Drafts," "Blue Studios," and the reprint of "Feminist Memoir Project;" correspondence with Dan Bouchard, Ron Silliman, Bob DuPlessis, David Antin, and an earlier note from deceased Eleanor Blau; notes on Tubingen Conference,Brian Kim Stefans, and John Banville.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes on Tubingen conference, Osvaldo Romberg conference, and "Drafts: Toll," as well as on Barrett Watten, Doggerel, Joan Retallack, and James Young; includes reflections on daughter Kore's college graduation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes on a trip to Auschwitz that were later incorporated into "Draft 81: Gap."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains material relating to "Drafts: Pitch," "Haavico," "Manhood," and beginning of "Purple Passages;" notes on Olson, Duncan, Creeley, and Zukofsky, as well as John Tranter, Leevi Lehto, Dean Poestakes, Ellen Willis, and James Young; includes pictures of Duplessis with Bob DuPlessis and friend Bruno.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "Draft 85: Hard Copy" and "7 Clay Songs; discussion of sexism with Anne Blonstein along with correspondence with Michael Davidson, Bob DuPlessis, DuPlessis' sister, Arden Reed, Susan Friedman, Meredith Quartermain, Bob Perelman, Michael Scrivener, Charles Bernstein, and Maria Damon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "Drafts," editing of a Palgrave book, as well as application processes for Guggenheim and National Humanities Center grants; notes on Bachmann, Alice Notely, Chris Tysh, Jean-Paul Auxemery, Bob DuPlessis, Imi Hwangbo, Eric Marty, Carla Biglitteri, and the death of Grace Paley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains material relating to "Gender Buttons" paper given at Feminism conference in Australia; free writing that is incorporated into "Draft 109: Wall Newspaper;" reflections from Bob DuPlessis' mother's funeral; notes on "Drafts."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains bibliography of poetry; notes on Darwish and Bill Cobbing event with Chris Cheek and Maggie O'Sullivan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "Trace Elements," Slought conference, Oppen conference, Long Poem conference; notes on Simon Jarvis, Peter Middleton, Renata Morresi, Jack Hirschman, and Carla Billiteri.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to the writing of "Draft 52: Midrash," "Draft 90: Excess," "Draft 101: Puppet Opera," a paper on T.S. Eliot and gender, and the Italian translation of "Drafts;" also includes notes on Peter Middleton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to the planning of "Drafts," including writing "Draft 86: Scarpbook," "Draft 87: Trace Elements;" discussion of "Purple Passages;" notes from Oppen conference; notes from time in Durham, North Carolina on fellowship with the National Humanities Center; notes on Joe Donahue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials from time on fellowship with the National Humanities Center in Durham, North Carolina; notes from Pittsburgh/Duquesne conference on feminism and modernism; writing of "Purple Passages," "Draft 90," "Draft 92," "Draft 95," and "Draft 99;" notes on Priscilla Wald, Joe Donahue, Carol Clover, Trevor Brenard, Fred Moten, Laura Marcus, David Herd, Peter Nicholls, Michael Davidson, Bob Perelman, and Nate Mackey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials related to "Lyric and Long Poem," "Draft 98," "Draft 99," "Draft 104," collage poems and Mail Art; notes on Erin Moure, Les Murray, Steve McCaffery, Tom Devaney, and Jean-Paul Auxemery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to Belladonna conference, new poetics conference at CUNY, and KWH conference; correspondence to Paul Jaussen; notes on Kathleen Fraser and Paul Zukofsky.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "Draft 110: Primer," the Collage Poems of "Drafts," and "Purple Passages;" notes on Duncan; correspondence with Bob Perelman, Forrest Gander, Betsy Hightower, and David Farwell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "Drafts;" notes on Christian Bok, Ashberry's Three Poems, Kaia Sand, Jules Boykoff, Mark Nowak, Jen Hofer, Susan Howe, Joe Donahue, Louis Armand, Smaro Kamboureli, Olsen and Creeley, Sianne Ngai, Banu Kapil, and KWH conference.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials on "The Long Poem" and "Drafts."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "Purple Passages" and "Drafts: Pitch;" notes on Ramazani, Maira Kalman, Zoe Strauss, Tan Dun, and Chris Hamilton-Emery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials regarding finishing "Purple Passages" and "Drafts;" notes on Duchamp; Kootneny conference with Meredith Quartermain; Olsen conference, including notes on David Herd, Stephen Collis, and Sharon Thesen; notes on Columbia Penn Poetics Initiative conference; notes on Betsy Hightower, Jacob Edmond, Frances Presley, Mairead Bryne, Vanessa Place, and Jean Day.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "Drafts," including notes on "Draft 108," "Draft 109," "Draft 113," and the final poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "Draft 105: Pilgrimmage," "Draft 108: Ballad and Gloss," "Draft 109: Wall Newspaper," "Purple Passages," and French translations of "Drafts;" notes from KWH and Feminist Studies Conference; notes on DuPlessis' late mother, Claire Moses, Sheila Hicks, Francie Shaw, C.A. Conrad, Chris Tysh, Ron Perelman, Renata Morresi, and David Herd; also includes reflections on retirement from Temple University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "Drafts," inlcuding "Draft 106" and "Draft 108; also includes reflections of retirement from Temple University and correspondence to Eric Keenaghan; notes on Oppen conference, including on Linda Oppen, Patrick Pritchett, and Dan Bouchard; reflections from trip to Turkey; correspondence to Kore from Bob DuPlessis and from Evy Varsamopoulou; notes on Coral Payne, Greg Brand, Paul Auster, Francie Shaw, Pavel Zoubuck, and Ellen Rose.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to the end of "Drafts," the publication processes of "Purple Passages" and "Draft 96: Velocity" chapbook, French translations, MLA conference paper on Duncan's H.D. book; notes from time as Distinguished Visiting Professor at University of Auckland, New Zealand; notes on Patrick Pritchett, Jean-Paul Auxemery and Chris Tysh, Michele Leggott, Wystan Curnow, Robert Sullivan, Tony Green, Jacob Edmonds, Ian Wedde, Sue Davis, Lisa Samuels, Ray Johnson, Caroline Bergvall, Liliane Lijne, Kurt Devrese, and Jean-Marie Gleize.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials from time as Distinguished Visiting Professor at University of New Zealand and summer in Italy; includes notes on Michele Leggott, Mark Fryer, Susan Davis, Len Lye, Colin McCahon, Ian Wedde, Robert Sullivan, Wystan Curnow, Susan Friedman at Temple University; notes on Objectivists conference in Italy, including Jean-Marie Gleize.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to trip to South Africa, including notes on national history.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "Woolfenstein," "Drafts," "Graphic Novella," and reflections on post-retirement life; notes on Juliette Lee, Maria Damon, Sue Wells, Hugh Grady, Paul Auster, Coral Payne and Greg Brand, daughter Kore and Dean Proestakes, and Peter and Meredith Quartermain.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to Poetic conference at University of Washington; notes on Corman-Zukofsky correspondence; notes on Stephen Collis, Joan Retallack, Linda Oppen, Jen Bervin, Jeremy Deller, and Amy Sadeo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "Interstices," "Drafts," and "The Oppens Remembered;" correspondence with Brian Reed, Bob DuPlessis, Michele Leggott, Maria Damon, and Mark Smith; notes on Tony Lopez, Andy Fitch, Brian Teare, and Carol Ascher.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to the organization of "Drafts," with notes on Notarikson and English to Italain translation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "Drafts," "Graphic Novella," "Objectivist" piece, and Cambridge History article; notes on Lisa Robertson, Niedecker, Joan Retallack, Paul Jaussen, Constance Cooper; correspondence with C.A. Conrad, Kathleen Fraser, Laynie Browne, and Clement Oudart.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to publication of "Drafts: Surge;" itinerary for Costa Rica; notes on Gertrude Stein's "Thank You: A Novel."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "Graphic Novella," "Cambridge History of Modern Literature," and the publication of "Drafts: Surge;" correspondence with Eric Keenaghan and Renata Morresi; notes on Bev Dahlen and John Tranter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials related to "Graphic Novella" and "Drafts;" correspondence with Clayton Eschleman regarding exclusion from Hoover anthology of post-modern poetry as well as with Lyn Hejinian, Cathy Wagner, Renata Morresi and Eleanora Tamburrini.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to writing sonnets, cosmological paper, and readings; includes notes on Dieci Bozze, Renata Morresi, Charles Alexander, Cynthia Hogue, and Jennifer Bartlett.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to finishing "Graphic Novella," and on "The Oppens Remembered;" notes on travels, Bob DuPlessis, and Joan Retallack.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to the publication process of "Graphic Novella," "Interstices," and "The Oppens Remembered;" correspondence with Jim Maynard, Dee Morris, Ron Perelman, Dan Bouchard, and Bob DuPlessis; notes from Toulouse conference and Skype event for Hank Lazar on Oppen, with student questions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to trip to France for the Toulouse conference; reflections on "Drafts;" notes from Bob DuPlessis' AHA conference.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "Days and Works" and "Poesis;" Djerassi Foundation; readings; notes on Carol Clover, Linda Oppen, and Bob DuPlessis; correspondence with Bob DuPlessis, Mike Katz, and DuPlessis' sister.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to Baudelaire poem written for Jo Milutis and review of Michele Leggott's "Heartland;" notes on daughter Kore and Dean Proestakes, Hugh Grady and Sue Wells, Don Giovanni, Carol Ascher, Bob Pittinger, and Dee Morris; trip notes from Vietnam and Cambodia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "The Oppens Remembered," "Churning the Ocean of Milk" collages and poems with "Mary Oppen" paper; notes from trip to Vietnam and Cambodia; correspondence with Jean-Paul Auxemery; notes on Bob DuPlessis and Peter Quartermain.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to "Long Poem" paper for MSA, "Churning the Ocean of Milk" collages, "The Oppens Remembered," and KWH event on Gertrude Stein; note on Mart Hart, Allen Fisher, Alison Bechdel, Elizabeh Wholey, Anne Tardos, Maurice Scully, and Mary Oppen and Linda Oppen; memorabilia from Vietnam and Cambodia trip.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to the publication process of "Churning the Ocean of Milk;" editing Linda Oppen's interview for "The Oppens Remembered;" writing "Eurydics;" "Reading Women's Poetry" essay for "Cambridge History of 20th Century American Women's Poetry;" collages; review of "Purple Passages;" notes on Linda Kinnahan, Mike Katz and Mary Dodge, Susan Howe, daughter Kore and Dean Proestakes.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials related to Rachel Blau DuPlessis's writings including drafts, research and subject files, notes, and other documentation related to the writing process.
